The bioactive constituents of Myristica fragrans include myristicin, elemicin, and safrole, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Myristica fragrans include nutmeg oil, nutmeg powder, and capsules, which are often used to treat digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote relaxation and sleep.
